title: [InstCombine] Canonicalize more geps with constant gep bases and constant offsets.

tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions 'at least one non-zero constant index' but all existing tests have variable indices. Fully constant outer GEP with nested constant base is not explicitly tested.

alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
@glob = global 4000 bytes, align 4

define ptr @all_constant_indices() {
entry:
  %inner = gep inbounds ptr @glob, 4000 x i64 0, 400 x i64 1, 40 x i64 2
  %outer = gep inbounds ptr %inner, 4000 x i64 0, 400 x i64 3, 40 x i64 1, 4 x i64 4
  ret ptr %outer
}
=>
@glob = global 4000 bytes, align 4

define ptr @all_constant_indices() {
entry:
  %__constexpr_0 = gep inbounds ptr @glob, 1 x i64 1736
  ret ptr %__constexpr_0
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:
  1 correct transformations
  0 incorrect transformations
  0 failed-to-prove transformations
  0 Alive2 errors

tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions nested GEPs but existing tests only cover 2 levels. This test has 3 levels of GEP nesting with constant offsets that should fold together.

alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
@glob = global 4000 bytes, align 4

define ptr @triple_nested_gep(i64 %x) {
entry:
  %gep1 = gep inbounds ptr @glob, 1 x i64 20
  %gep2 = gep inbounds ptr %gep1, 1 x i64 16
  %gep3 = gep inbounds ptr %gep2, 4000 x i64 0, 400 x i64 %x, 40 x i64 1, 4 x i64 3
  ret ptr %gep3
}
=>
@glob = global 4000 bytes, align 4

define ptr @triple_nested_gep(i64 %x) {
entry:
  %gep3.idx = mul nsw i64 %x, 400
  %__constexpr_0 = gep inbounds ptr @glob, 1 x i64 88
  %gep3 = gep ptr %__constexpr_0, 1 x i64 %gep3.idx
  ret ptr %gep3
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:
  1 correct transformations
  0 incorrect transformations
  0 failed-to-prove transformations
  0 Alive2 errors

tests.outside_scope_reason: All existing nested GEP tests show exactly 2 levels of nesting. This tests 3 levels of nested GEPs with constant offsets, verifying the optimization chains through multiple constant GEP bases. The summary doesn't specify depth limits, making this an untested edge case.

alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
@glob = global 4000 bytes, align 4

define ptr @triple_nested_constant_gep(i64 %x) {
entry:
  %gep1 = gep ptr @glob, 1 x i64 100
  %gep2 = gep ptr %gep1, 1 x i64 50
  %gep3 = gep ptr %gep2, 4000 x i64 0, 400 x i64 %x, 40 x i64 3, 4 x i64 1
  ret ptr %gep3
}
=>
@glob = global 4000 bytes, align 4

define ptr @triple_nested_constant_gep(i64 %x) {
entry:
  %gep3.idx = mul i64 %x, 400
  %__constexpr_0 = gep inbounds ptr @glob, 1 x i64 274
  %gep3 = gep ptr %__constexpr_0, 1 x i64 %gep3.idx
  ret ptr %gep3
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:
  1 correct transformations
  0 incorrect transformations
  0 failed-to-prove transformations
  0 Alive2 errors
